scenarios.title: Medical Imaging and Segmentation Workflows
scenarios.when_to_read: Medical-image segmentation, CT/MR anatomical structure masks, nnU-Net-backed inference, NIfTI/DICOM handling, and TotalSegmentator package automation.
scenarios.role: Use this repo skill for TotalSegmentator-specific task discovery, CLI/API segmentation, runtime setup, output parsing, DICOM formatting, auxiliary analysis, and retraining boundaries.
scenarios.read_when: User mentions TotalSegmentator, totalsegmentator, TotalSegmentator CLI, totalseg_info, totalseg_combine_masks, CT/MR segmentation, --roi_subset, --report, statistics.json, DICOM SEG, RTSTRUCT, model weights, license, body stats, contrast phase, modality prediction, Evans index, or TotalSegmentator nnU-Net retraining.
scenarios.best_for: Planning and troubleshooting TotalSegmentator runs; discovering valid tasks/classes; building reproducible segmentation commands; parsing run reports/statistics; configuring weights/licenses/offline use; validating DICOM/NIfTI format choices; using secondary analysis CLIs; understanding advanced retraining/evaluation workflows.
scenarios.avoid_when: Use a general MONAI, nnU-Net, TorchIO, or medical-imaging skill when the task is not TotalSegmentator-specific; avoid using this skill as clinical validation guidance or as a generic medical diagnosis tool.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `totalsegmentator` when the request names TotalSegmentator or one of its CLIs/APIs. For overlapping medical imaging work, prefer `totalsegmentator` only when TotalSegmentator behavior, registry tasks/classes, reports, weights/licenses, or output utilities are central; otherwise choose the package-specific medical-imaging skill for the named framework.
